text: Ṭā, Hā.
tl: Taa-Haa

text: We have not sent down to you the Qur’ān that you be distressed
tl: Maaa anzalnaa 'alaikal Qur-aana litashqaaa

text: But only as a reminder for those who fear [Allāh] -
tl: Illaa tazkiratal limany yakhshaa

text: A revelation from He who created the earth and highest heavens,
tl: Tanzeelan mimman khalaqal arda was-samaawaatil 'ulaa

text: The Most Merciful [who is] above the Throne established.
tl: Ar-Rahmaanu 'alal 'Arshis tawaa

text: To Him belongs what is in the heavens and what is on the earth and what is between them and what is under the soil.
tl: Lahoo maa fis samaawaati Wa-maa fil ardi Wa-maa bainahumaa Wa-maa tahtas saraa

text: And if you speak aloud - then indeed, He knows the secret and what is [even] more hidden.
tl: Wa-in tajhar bilqawli fainnahoo ya'lamus sirra Wa-akhfaa

text: Allāh - there is no deity except Him. To Him belong the best names.
tl: Allaahu laaa ilaaha illaa huwa lahul asmaa'ul husnaa

text: And has the story of Moses reached you? -
tl: Wa-hal ataaka hadeesu Moosa

text: When he saw a fire and said to his family, \"Stay here; indeed, I have perceived a fire; perhaps I can bring you a torch or find at the fire some guidance.\"
tl: Iz ra-aa naaran faqaala li-ahlihim kusooo inneee aanastu naaral la'alleee aateekum minhaa biqabasin aw ajidu 'alan naari hudaa

text: And when he came to it, he was called, \"O Moses,
tl: Falammaaa ataahaa noodiya yaa-Moosaa

text: Indeed, I am your Lord, so remove your sandals. Indeed, you are in the blessed valley of Ṭuwā.
tl: Inneee Ana Rabbuka fakhla' na'laika innaka bilwaadil muqaddasi Tuwaa

text: And I have chosen you, so listen to what is revealed [to you].
tl: Wa anakhtartuka fastami' limaa yoohaa

text: Indeed, I am Allāh. There is no deity except Me, so worship Me and establish prayer for My remembrance.
tl: Innaneee Anal laahu laaa ilaaha illaa Ana fa'budnee Wa-aqimis salaata lizikree

text: Indeed, the Hour is coming - I almost conceal it - so that every soul may be recompensed according to that for which it strives.
tl: Innas Saa'ata aatiyatun akaadu ukhfeehaa litujzaa kullu nafsin bimaa tas'aa

text: So do not let one avert you from it who does not believe in it and follows his desire, for you [then] would perish.
tl: Falaa yasuddannaka 'anhaa mal laa yu'minu bihaa wattaba'a hawaahu fatardaa

text: And what is that in your right hand, O Moses?\"
tl: Wa-maa tilka bi-yamee-nika yaa-Moosaa

text: He said, \"It is my staff; I lean upon it, and I bring down leaves for my sheep and I have therein other uses.\"
tl: Qaala hiya 'asaaya atawakka'u alaihaa Wa-ahushshu bihaa 'alaa ghanamee Wa-liya feehaa ma-aaribu ukhraa

text: [Allāh] said, \"Throw it down, O Moses.\"
tl: Qaala alqihaa yaa-Moosaa

text: So he threw it down, and thereupon it was a snake, moving swiftly.
tl: Fa-alqaahaa fa-izaa hiya haiyatun tas'aa

text: [Allāh] said, \"Seize it and fear not; We will return it to its former condition.
tl: Qaala khuzhaa Wa-laa ta-khaf sanu'eeduhaa seeratahal oolaa

text: And draw in your hand to your side; it will come out white without disease - another sign,
tl: Wadmum yadaka ilaa janaahika takhruj baidaaa'a min ghairi sooo'in Aayatan ukhraa

text: That We may show you [some] of Our greater signs.
tl: Linuriyaka min Aayaatinal Kubra

text: Go to Pharaoh. Indeed, he has transgressed [i.e., tyrannized].\"
tl: Izhab ilaa Fir'awna innahoo taghaa

text: [Moses] said, \"My Lord, expand [i.e., relax] for me my breast [with assurance]
tl: Qaala Rabbish rah lee sadree

text: And ease for me my task
tl: Wa-yassir leee amree

text: And untie the knot from my tongue
tl: Wahlul 'uqdatan milli saanee

text: That they may understand my speech.
tl: Yafqahoo qawlee

text: And appoint for me a minister [i.e., assistant] from my family -
tl: Waj'al lee wazeeran min ahlee

text: Aaron, my brother.
tl: Haaroona akhee

text: Increase through him my strength
tl: Ushdud biheee azree

text: And let him share my task
tl: Wa-ashrik-hu feee amree

text: That we may exalt You much
tl: Kai nusabbihaka kaseeraa

text: And remember You much.
tl: Wa-nazkuraka kaseeraa

text: Indeed, You are of us ever Seeing.\"
tl: Innaka kunta binaa baseeraa

text: [Allāh] said, \"You have been granted your request, O Moses.
tl: Qaala qad ooteeta su'laka yaa-Moosaa

text: And We had already conferred favor upon you another time,
tl: Wa-laqad manannaa 'alaika marratan ukhraaa

text: When We inspired to your mother what We inspired,
tl: Iz awhainaaa ilaaa ummika maa yoohaaa

text: [Saying], 'Cast him into the chest and cast it into the river, and the river will throw it onto the bank; there will take him an enemy to Me and an enemy to him.' And I bestowed upon you love from Me that you would be brought up under My eye [i.e., observation and care].
tl: Ani qzifeehi fit Taabooti fa-qzifeehi fil yammi fal-yul-qihil yammu bis-saahili ya'khuzhu 'aduwwul lee Wa-'aduwwul lah; Wa-alqaitu 'alaika mahabbatan minnee Wa-litusna'a 'alaa 'ainee

text: [And We favored you] when your sister went and said, 'Shall I direct you to someone who will be responsible for him?' So We restored you to your mother that she might be content and not grieve. And you killed someone, but We saved you from retaliation and tried you with a [severe] trial. And you remained [some] years among the people of Madyan. Then you came [here] at the decreed time, O Moses.
tl: Iz tamsheee ukhtuka fataqoolu hal adullukum 'alaa maien yakfuluhoo faraja-'naaka ilaaa ummika kai taqarra 'ainuhaa Wa-laa tahzan; Wa-qatalta nafsan fanajjainaaka minal ghammi Wa-fatannaaka futoonaa; falabista sineena feee ahli Madyana summa ji'ta 'alaa qadarien yaa-Moosa

text: And I produced you for Myself.
tl: Wastana'-tuka linafsee

text: Go, you and your brother, with My signs and do not slacken in My remembrance.
tl: Izhab anta Wa-akhooka bi-Aayaatee Wa-laa taniyaa fee zikree

text: Go, both of you, to Pharaoh. Indeed, he has transgressed.
tl: Izhabaaa ilaa Fir'awna innahoo taghaa

text: And speak to him with gentle speech that perhaps he may be reminded or fear [Allāh].\"
tl: Faqoolaa lahoo qawlal laiyinal la-allahoo yatazakkkaru 'aw yakhshaa

text: They said, \"Our Lord, indeed we are afraid that he will hasten [punishment] against us or that he will transgress.\"
tl: Qaalaa Rabbanaaa innanaa nakhaafu aien yafruta 'alainaaa aw ai yatghaa

text: [Allāh] said, \"Fear not. Indeed, I am with you both; I hear and I see.
tl: Qaala laa takhaafaaa innanee ma'akumaa asma'u Wa-araa

text: So go to him and say, 'Indeed, we are messengers of your Lord, so send with us the Children of Israel and do not torment them. We have come to you with a sign from your Lord. And peace will be upon he who follows the guidance.
tl: Faatiyaahu faqoolaaa innaa Rasoolaa Rabbika fa-arsil ma'anaa Banee Israaa'eela Wa-laa tu'azzibhum qad ji'naaka bi-Aayatim mir Rabbika wassa-laamu 'alaa manit taba'al hudaa

text: Indeed, it has been revealed to us that the punishment will be upon whoever denies and turns away.'\"
tl: Innaa qad oohiya ilainaaa annnal 'azaaba 'alaa man kaz-zaba Wa-tawalla

text: [Pharaoh] said, \"So who is the Lord of you two, O Moses?\"
tl: Qaala famar Rabbu-kumaa yaa-Moosa

text: He said, \"Our Lord is He who gave each thing its form and then guided [it].\"
tl: Qaala Rabbunal lazeee a'taa kulla shai'in khalqahoo summa hadaa

text: [Pharaoh] said, \"Then what is the case of the former generations?\"
tl: Qaala famaa baalul quroonil oolaa

text: [Moses] said, \"The knowledge thereof is with my Lord in a record. My Lord neither errs nor forgets.\"
tl: Qaala 'ilmuhaa 'inda Rabee fee kitaab, laa yadillu Rabbee Wa-laa yansaa

text: [It is He] who has made for you the earth as a bed [spread out] and inserted therein for you roadways and sent down from the sky, rain and produced thereby categories of various plants.
tl: Allazee ja'ala lakumul arda mahdanw Wa-salaka lakum feehaa subulanw Wa-anzala minas samaaa'i maaa'an fa-akhrajnaa biheee azwaajam min nabaatin shatta

text: Eat [therefrom] and pasture your livestock. Indeed in that are signs for those of intelligence.
tl: Kuloo war'aw an'aamakum; inna fee zaalika la-Aayaatil li-ulin nuhaa

text: From it [i.e., the earth] We created you, and into it We will return you, and from it We will extract you another time.
tl: Minhaa khalaqnaakum Wa-feehaa nu'eedukum Wa-minhaa nukhrijukum taaratan ukhraa

text: And We certainly showed him [i.e., Pharaoh] Our signs - all of them - but he denied and refused.
tl: Wa-laqad arainaahu Aayaatinaa kullahaa fakaz-zaba Wa-abaa

text: He said, \"Have you come to us to drive us out of our land with your magic, O Moses?
tl: Qaala aji'tanaa litukhri-janaa min ardinaa bisihrika yaa-Moosa

text: Then we will surely bring you magic like it, so make between us and you an appointment, which we will not fail to keep and neither will you, in a place assigned.\"
tl: Falanaatiyannaka bisihrim mislihee faj'al bainanaa Wa-bainaka maw'idal laa nukhlifuhoo nahnu Wa-laaa anta makaanan suwaa

text: [Moses] said, \"Your appointment is on the day of the festival when the people assemble at mid-morning.\"
tl: Qaala maw'idukum yawmuz zeenati Wa-ai yuhsharan naasu duhaa

text: So Pharaoh went away, put together his plan, and then came [to Moses].
tl: Fatawallaa Fir'awnu fajjama'a kaidahoo summa ataa

text: Moses said to them [i.e., the magicians summoned by Pharaoh], \"Woe to you! Do not invent a lie against Allāh or He will exterminate you with a punishment; and he has failed who invents [such falsehood].\"
tl: Qaala lahum Moosaa wailakum laa taftaroo 'alal laahi kaziban fa-yus-hitakum bi-'azaab, Wa-qad khaaba manif taraa

text: So they disputed over their affair among themselves and concealed their private conversation.
tl: Fatanaaza'ooo amrahum bainahum Wa-asarrun najwaa

text: They said, \"Indeed, these are two magicians who want to drive you out of your land with their magic and do away with your most exemplary way [i.e., religion or tradition].
tl: Qaalooo in haaazaani lasaahiraani yureedaani aien yukhrijaakum min ardikum bisihrihimaa Wa-yazhabaa bitareeqatikumul muslaa

text: So resolve upon your plan and then come [forward] in line. And he has succeeded today who overcomes.\"
tl: Fa-ajmi'oo kaidakum summa' too saffaa; Wa-qad aflahal yawma manis ta'laa

text: They said, \"O Moses, either you throw or we will be the first to throw.\"
tl: Qaaloo yaa-Moosaaa immaaa an tulqiya Wa-immaaa an nakoona awala man alqaa

text: He said, \"Rather, you throw.\" And suddenly their ropes and staffs seemed to him from their magic that they were moving [like snakes].
tl: Qaala bal alqoo fa-izaa hibaaluhum Wa-'isiyyuhum yukhaiyalu ilaihi min sihrihim annahaa tas'aa

text: And he sensed within himself apprehension, did Moses.
tl: Fa-awjasa fee nafsihee kheefatam Moosa

text: We [i.e., Allāh] said, \"Fear not. Indeed, it is you who are superior.
tl: Qulnaa laa takhaf innaka antal a'laa

text: And throw what is in your right hand; it will swallow up what they have crafted. What they have crafted is but the trick of a magician, and the magician will not succeed wherever he is.\"
tl: Wa-alqi maa fee yamee-nika talqaf maa sana'oo; innamaa sana'oo kaidu saahir; Wa-laa yuflihus saahiru haisu ataa

text: So the magicians fell down in prostration. They said, \"We have believed in the Lord of Aaron and Moses.\"
tl: Fa-ulqiyas saharatu sujjadan qaalooo aamannaa bi-Rabbi Haaroona Wa-Moosa

text: [Pharaoh] said, \"You believed him [i.e., Moses] before I gave you permission. Indeed, he is your leader who has taught you magic. So I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will crucify you on the trunks of palm trees, and you will surely know which of us is more severe in [giving] punishment and more enduring.\"
tl: Qaala aamantum lahoo qabla an aazana lakum; innahoo lakabeerukumul lazee 'allama-kumus sihra fala-uqatti'anna aidiyakum Wa-arjulakum min khilaafinw Wa-la-usallibannakum fee juzoo'in nakhli Wa-lata'lamunna aiyunaaa ashaddu 'azaabanw Wa-abqaa

text: They said, \"Never will we prefer you over what has come to us of clear proofs and [over] He who created us. So decree whatever you are to decree. You can only decree for this worldly life.
tl: Qaaloo lan nu'siraka 'alaa maa jaaa'anaa minal baiyinaati wallazee fataranaa faqdi maaa anta qaad; innamaa taqdee haazihil hayaatad dunyaa

text: Indeed, we have believed in our Lord that He may forgive us our sins and what you compelled us [to do] of magic. And Allāh is better and more enduring.\"
tl: Innaaa aamannaa bi-Rabbinaa liyaghfira lanaa khataayaanaa Wa-maaa akrahtanaa 'alaihi minas sihr; wallaahu khairunw Wa-abqaa

text: Indeed, whoever comes to his Lord as a criminal - indeed, for him is Hell; he will neither die therein nor live.
tl: Innahoo mai ya'ti Rabbahoo mujriman fa-inna lahoo Jahannama laa yamootu feehaa Wa-laa yahyaa

text: But whoever comes to Him as a believer having done righteous deeds - for those will be the highest degrees [in position]:
tl: Wa-main ya'tihee mu'minan qad 'amilas saalihaati fa-ulaaa'ika lahumud darajaatul 'ulaa

text: Gardens of perpetual residence beneath which rivers flow, wherein they abide eternally. And that is the reward of one who purifies himself.
tl: Jannaatu 'Adnin tajree min tahtihal anhaaru khaalideena feehaa; Wa-zaalika jazaaa'u man tazakka

text: And We had inspired to Moses, \"Travel by night with My servants and strike for them a dry path through the sea; you will not fear being overtaken [by Pharaoh] nor be afraid [of drowning].\"
tl: Wa-laqad awhainaaa ilaa Moosaaa an asri bi'ibaadee fadrib lahum tareeqan fil bahri yabasal laa takhaafu darakanw Wa-laa takhshaa

text: So Pharaoh pursued them with his soldiers, and there covered them from the sea that which covered them,
tl: Fa-atba'ahum Fir'awnu bijunoodihee faghashiyahum minal yammmi maa ghashi-yahum

text: And Pharaoh led his people astray and did not guide [them].
tl: wa-adalla fir'awnu qawmahoo Wa-maa hadaa

text: O Children of Israel, We delivered you from your enemy, and We made an appointment with you at the right side of the mount, and We sent down to you manna and quails,
tl: Yaa-Baneee Israaa'eela qad anjainaakum min 'aduw-wikum Wa-wa'adnaakum jaanibat Tooril aimana Wa-nazzalnaa 'alaikumul Manna was-Salwaa

text: [Saying], \"Eat from the good things with which We have provided you and do not transgress [or oppress others] therein, lest My anger should descend upon you. And he upon whom My anger descends has certainly fallen [i.e., perished].\"
tl: Kuloo min taiyibaati maa razaqnaakum Wa-laa tatghaw feehi fa-yahilla 'alaikum ghadabee Wa-mai yahlil 'alaihi ghadabee faqad hawaa

text: But indeed, I am the Perpetual Forgiver of whoever repents and believes and does righteousness and then continues in guidance.
tl: Wa-innee la-Ghaffaarul liman taaba Wa-aamana Wa-'amila saalihan summah tadaa

text: [Allāh said], \"And what made you hasten from your people, O Moses?\"
tl: Wa-maaa a'jalaka 'an qawmika yaa-Moosa

text: He said, \"They are close upon my tracks, and I hastened to You, my Lord, that You be pleased.\"
tl: Qaala hum ulaaa'i 'alaaa asaree Wa-'ajiltu ilaika Rabbi litardaa

text: [Allāh] said, \"But indeed, We have tried your people after you [departed], and the Sāmirī has led them astray.\"
tl: Qaala fa-innaa qad fatannaa qawmaka mim ba'dika Wa-adallahumus Saamiriyy

text: So Moses returned to his people, angry and grieved. He said, \"O my people, did your Lord not make you a good promise? Then, was the time [of its fulfillment] too long for you, or did you wish that wrath from your Lord descend upon you, so you broke your promise [of obedience] to me?\"
tl: Faraja'a Moosaaa ilaa qawmihee ghadbaana asifaa; qaala yaa-qawmi alam ya'idkum Rabbukum wa'dan hasanaa; afataala 'alaikumul 'ahdu am arattum ai yahilla 'alaikum ghadabum mir Rabbikum fa-akhlaftum maw'idee

text: They said, \"We did not break our promise to you by our will, but we were made to carry burdens from the ornaments of the people [of Pharaoh], so we threw them [into the fire], and thus did the Sāmirī throw.\"
tl: Qaaloo maaa akhlafnaa maw'idaka bimalkinna Wa-laakinna hummilnaaa awzaaram min zeenatil qawmi faqazafnaahaa fakazaalika alqas Saamiriyy

text: And he extracted for them [the statue of] a calf which had a lowing sound, and they said, \"This is your god and the god of Moses, but he forgot.\"
tl: Fa-akhraja lahum 'ijlan jasadal lahoo khuwaarun faqaaloo haazaaa ilaahukum Wa-ilaahu Moosaa fanasee

text: Did they not see that it could not return to them any speech [i.e., response] and that it did not possess for them any harm or benefit?
tl: Afalaa yarawna allaa yarji'u ilaihim qawlanw Wa-laa yamliku lahum darranw Wa-laa naf'aa

text: And Aaron had already told them before [the return of Moses], \"O my people, you are only being tested by it, and indeed, your Lord is the Most Merciful, so follow me and obey my order.\"
tl: Wa-laqad qaala lahum Haaroonu min qablu yaa-qawmi innamaa futintum bihee Wa-inna Rabbakumur Rahmaanu fattabi'oonee Wa-atee'ooo amree

text: They said, \"We will never cease being devoted to it [i.e., the calf] until Moses returns to us.\"
tl: Qaaloo lan nabraha 'alaihi 'aakifeena hattaa yarji'a ilainaa Moosaa

text: [Moses] said, \"O Aaron, what prevented you, when you saw them going astray,
tl: Qaala Yaa-Haaroonu maa mana-'aka iz ra-aitahum dallooo

text: From following me? Then have you disobeyed my order?\"
tl: Allaa tattabi'ani afa'asaita amree

text: [Aaron] said, \"O son of my mother, do not seize [me] by my beard or by my head. Indeed, I feared that you would say, 'You caused division among the Children of Israel, and you did not observe [or await] my word.'\"
tl: Qaala yabna'umma laa ta'khuz bi-lihyatee Wa-laa bi-ra'see innee khasheetu an taqoola farraqta baina Baneee Israaa'eela Wa-lam tarqub qawlee

text: [Moses] said, \"And what is your case, O Sāmirī?\"
tl: Qaala famaa khatbuka yaa-Saamiriyy

text: He said, \"I saw what they did not see, so I took a handful [of dust] from the track of the messenger and threw it, and thus did my soul entice me.\"
tl: Qaala basurtu bimaa lam yabsuroo bihee faqabadtu qabdatam min asarir Rasooli fanabaztuhaa Wa-kazaalika sawwalat lee nafsee

text: [Moses] said, \"Then go. And indeed, it is [decreed] for you in [this] life to say, 'No contact.' And indeed, you have an appointment [in the Hereafter] you will not fail to keep. And look at your 'god' to which you remained devoted. We will surely burn it and blow it [i.e., its ashes] into the sea with a blast.
tl: Qaala fazhab fa-inna laka fil hayaati an taqoola laa misaasa Wa-inna laka maw'idal lan tukhlafahoo wanzur ilaaa ilaahikal lazee zalta 'alaihi 'aakifaa; la-nuharriqannahoo thumma la-nansifannahoo fil yammi nasfaa

text: Your god is only Allāh, except for whom there is no deity. He has encompassed all things in knowledge.\"
tl: Innamaaa ilaahu-kumul laahul lazee laa ilaaha illaa Hoo; wasi'a kulla shai'in ilmaa

text: Thus, [O Muḥammad], We relate to you from the news of what has preceded. And We have certainly given you from Us a message [i.e., the Qur’ān].
tl: Kazaalika naqussu 'alaika min anbaaa'i maa qad sabaq; Wa-qad aatainaaka mil ladunnaa Zikraa

text: Whoever turns away from it - then indeed, he will bear on the Day of Resurrection a burden [i.e., great sin],
tl: Man a'rada 'anhu, fa-innahoo yahmilu Yawmal Qiyaamati wizraa

text: [Abiding] eternally therein, and evil it is for them on the Day of Resurrection as a load -
tl: Khaalideena feehi Wa-saaa'a lahum Yawmal Qiyaamati himlaa

text: The Day the Horn will be blown. And We will gather the criminals, that Day, blue-eyed.
tl: Yawma yunfakhu fissoori Wa-nahshurul mujrimeena Yawma 'izin zurqaa

text: They will murmur among themselves, \"You remained not but ten [days in the world].\"
tl: Yatakhaafatoona bainahum il labistum illaa 'ashraa

text: We are most knowing of what they say when the best of them in manner [i.e., wisdom or speech] will say, \"You remained not but one day.\"
tl: nahnu a'lamu bimaa yaqooloona iz yaqoolu amsaluhum tareeqatan il labistum illaa yawmaa

text: And they ask you about the mountains, so say, \"My Lord will blow them away with a blast.
tl: Wa-yas'aloonaka 'anil jibaali faqul yansifuhaa Rabbee nasfaa

text: And He will leave it [i.e., the earth] a level plain;
tl: Fa-yazaruhaa qaa'an safsafaa

text: You will not see therein a depression or an elevation.\"
tl: Laa taraa feehaa 'iwajanw Wa-laaa amtaa

text: That Day, they [i.e., everyone] will follow [the call of] the Caller [with] no deviation therefrom, and [all] voices will be stilled before the Most Merciful, so you will not hear except a whisper [of footsteps].
tl: Yawma-izien yattabi'oonad daa'iya laa 'iwaja lahoo Wa-khasha'atil aswaatu lir-Rahmaani falaa tasma'u illaa hamsaa

text: That Day, no intercession will benefit except [that of] one to whom the Most Merciful has given permission and has accepted his word.
tl: Yawma-'izil laa tanfa'ush shafaa'atu illaa man azina lahur Rahmaanu Wa-radiya lahoo qawlaa

text: He [i.e., Allāh] knows what is [presently] before them and what will be after them, but they do not encompass it [i.e., what He knows] in knowledge.
tl: Ya'lamu maa bainaa aideehim Wa-maa khalfahum Wa-laa yuheetoona bihee 'ilmaa

text: And [all] faces will be humbled before the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining. And he will have failed who carries injustice.
tl: Wa-'anatil wujoohu lil-Haiiyil Qaiyoomi Wa-qad khaaba man hamala zulmaa

text: But he who does of righteous deeds while he is a believer - he will neither fear injustice nor deprivation.
tl: Wa-main ya'mal minas saalihaati Wa-huwa mu'minun falaa yakhaafu zulmanw Wa-laa hadmaa

text: And thus We have sent it down as an Arabic Qur’ān and have diversified therein the warnings that perhaps they will avoid [sin] or it would cause them remembrance.
tl: Wa-kazaalika anzalnaahu Qur-aanan 'Arabiyyanw Wa-sarrafnaa fee-hi minal wa'eedi la'allahum yattaqoona aw yuhdisu lahum zikraa

text: So high [above all] is Allāh, the Sovereign, the Truth. And, [O Muḥammad], do not hasten with [recitation of] the Qur’ān before its revelation is completed to you, and say, \"My Lord, increase me in knowledge.\"
tl: Fata'aalal laahul Malikul Haqq; Wa-laa ta'jal bil-Quraani min qabli aien yuqdaaa ilaika wahyuhoo Wa-qur Rabbi zidnee 'ilmaa

text: And We had already taken a promise from Adam before, but he forgot; and We found not in him determination.
tl: Wa-laqad 'ahidnaaa ilaaa Aadama min qablu fanasiya Wa-lam najid lahoo 'azmaa

text: And [mention] when We said to the angels, \"Prostrate to Adam,\" and they prostrated, except Iblees; he refused.
tl: Wa-iz qulnaa lilma-laaa'ikatis judoo li-Aadama fasajadooo illaaa Ibleesa; abaa

text: So We said, \"O Adam, indeed this is an enemy to you and to your wife. Then let him not remove you from Paradise so you would suffer.
tl: Faqulnaa yaaa-Aadamu inna haazaa 'aduwwul laka Wa-lizawjika falaa yukhrijan-nakumaa minal Jannati fatashqaa

text: Indeed, it is [promised] for you not to be hungry therein or be unclothed.
tl: Innaa laka allaa tajoo'a feeha Wa-laa ta'raa

text: And indeed, you will not be thirsty therein or be hot from the sun.\"
tl: Wa-annaka laa tazma'u feehaa Wa-laa tadhaa

text: Then Satan whispered to him; he said, \"O Adam, shall I direct you to the tree of eternity and possession that will not deteriorate?\"
tl: Fa-waswasa ilaihish Shaitaanu qaala yaaa-Aadamu hal adulluka 'alaa shajaratil khuldi Wa-mulkil laa yablaa

text: And they [i.e., Adam and his wife] ate of it, and their private parts became apparent to them, and they began to fasten over themselves from the leaves of Paradise. And Adam disobeyed his Lord and erred.
tl: Fa-akalaa minhaa fabadat lahumaa saw-aatuhumaa Wa-tafiqaa yakhsifaani 'alaihimaa minw waraqil jannah; Wa-'asaaa Aadamu Rabbahoo faghawaa

text: Then his Lord chose him and turned to him in forgiveness and guided [him].
tl: Summaj tabbahu Rabbuhoo fataaba 'alaihi Wa-hadaa

text: [Allāh] said, \"Descend from it [i.e., Paradise] - all, [your descendants] being enemies to one another. And if there should come to you guidance from Me - then whoever follows My guidance will neither go astray [in the world] nor suffer [in the Hereafter].
tl: Qaalah bita minhaa jamee'am ba'dukum liba'din 'aduww; fa-immaa ya'tiyannakum minnee hudan famanit taba'a hudaaya falaa yadillu Wa-laa yashqaa

text: And whoever turns away from My remembrance - indeed, he will have a depressed [i.e., difficult] life, and We will gather [i.e., raise] him on the Day of Resurrection blind.\"
tl: Wa-man a'rada 'an Zikree fa-inna lahoo ma'eeshatan dankanw Wa-nahshuruhoo Yawmal Qiyaamati a'maa

text: He will say, \"My Lord, why have you raised me blind while I was [once] seeing?\"
tl: Qaala Rabbi lima hashar-tanee a'maa Wa-qad kuntu baseeraa

text: [Allāh] will say, \"Thus did Our signs come to you, and you forgot [i.e., disregarded] them; and thus will you this Day be forgotten.\"
tl: Qaala kazaalika atatka Aayaatunaa fanaseetahaa Wa-kazaalikal Yawma tunsaa

text: And thus do We recompense he who transgressed and did not believe in the signs of his Lord. And the punishment of the Hereafter is more severe and more enduring.
tl: Wa-kazaalika najzee man asrafa Wa-lam yu'min bi-Aayaati Rabbih; Wa-la'azaabul Aakhirati ashaddu Wa-abqaa

text: Then, has it not become clear to them how many generations We destroyed before them as they walk among their dwellings? Indeed in that are signs for those of intelligence.
tl: Afalam yahdi lahum kam ahlaknaa qablahum minal qurooni yamshoona fee masaakinihim; inna fee zaalika la-Aayaatil li-ulin nuhaa

text: And if not for a word that preceded from your Lord, it [i.e., punishment] would have been an obligation [due immediately], and [if not for] a specified term [decreed].
tl: Wa-law-laa Kalimatun sabaqat mir Rabbika lakaana lizaamanw Wa-'ajalun musammaa

text: So be patient over what they say and exalt [Allāh] with praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before its setting; and during periods of the night [exalt Him] and at the ends of the day, that you may be satisfied.
tl: Fasbir 'alaa maa yaqooloona Wa-sabbih bihamdi Rabbika qabla tuloo'ish shamsi Wa-qabla ghuroobihaa Wa-min aanaaa'il laili fasabbih Wa-atraafan nahaari la-'allaka tardaa

text: And do not extend your eyes toward that by which We have given enjoyment to [some] categories of them, [its being but] the splendor of worldly life by which We test them. And the provision of your Lord is better and more enduring.
tl: Wa-laa tamuddanna 'ainaika ilaa ma matta'na biheee azwajam minhum zahratal hayaatid dunya linaftinahum feeh; Wa-rizqu Rabbika khairunw Wa-abqaa

text: And enjoin prayer upon your family [and people] and be steadfast therein. We ask you not for provision; We provide for you, and the [best] outcome is for [those of] righteousness.
tl: Wa'mur ahlaka bis-Salaati wastabir 'alaihaa laa nas'aluka rizqaa; nahnu narzuquk; wal-'aaqibatu littaqwaa

text: And they say, \"Why does he not bring us a sign from his Lord?\" Has there not come to them evidence of what was in the former scriptures?
tl: Wa-qaaloo law-laa ya'teenaa bi-aayatin mir Rabbih; awa-lam ta'tihim baiyyinatu maa fis suhufil oolaa

text: And if We had destroyed them with a punishment before him, they would have said, \"Our Lord, why did You not send to us a messenger so we could have followed Your verses [i.e., teachings] before we were humiliated and disgraced?\"
tl: Wa-law annaaa ahlaknaahum bi-'azaabin min qablihee laqaaloo Rabbanaa law-laaa arsalta ilainaa Rasoolan fanattabi'a Aayaatika min qabli an nazilla Wa-nakhzaa

text: Say, \"Each [of us] is waiting; so wait. For you will know who are the companions of the sound path and who is guided.\"
tl: Qul kullum mutarabbisun fa-tarabbasoo fa-sa-ta'lamoona man Ashaabus Siraatis Sawiyyi Wa-manih tadaa
